story_clusters.headline: RBI MPC begins policy meet amid West Asia crisis; decision on Wednesday
story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has begun its policy meeting amid the West Asia crisis, with a decision expected on Wednesday. RBI Governor has cautioned that the West Asia conflict could impede growth and increase imported inflation, while the central bank is also addressing concerns over a weak rupee. The RBI has previously cut rates by a total of 125 basis points since February 2025.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has invited public comments on draft Amendment Directions regarding the review of the methodology for identifying NBFC-UL (Upper Layer Non-Banking Financial Companies). The draft proposes defining upper-layer NBFCs based on an absolute asset size of ₹1 lakh crore or more and includes government-owned NBFCs, including PSUs, in this category.

story_clusters.summary: Multiple sources report that a US-Iran ceasefire agreement, referred to as a "calibrated pause," has been reached. The pact is expected to reduce tensions between the two nations, with economic impacts including potential declines in corn and oil prices due to eased supply concerns. Analysts note the fragility of the arrangement as markets react to the developments.

story_clusters.summary: Justice Yashwant Varma withdrew from a judges' inquiry committee (JIC) amid allegations of financial irregularities, stating in a letter that "history will record the unfairness." This marks the third instance in Indian history where a judge resigned before facing impeachment proceedings. Multiple reports confirm his departure from the JIC following the disclosure of cash found in his residence.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has issued a circular introducing guidelines to expedite cross-border inward payments, including faster alerts and one-hour reconciliation for banks. Additionally, the RBI released a Discussion Paper proposing safeguards in digital payments, such as a one-hour delay and enhanced customer controls, to address rising frauds. These measures aim to improve efficiency and security in digital transactions.

Jaishankar is set to visit the UAE to discuss the Emirati stance on the West Asia crisis and Pakistan, while Oil Minister Piyush Goyal has met with Kuwait and UAE officials to restore trade and energy flows. Additionally, India is finalizing an agreement to supply oil and gas to Mauritius.

story_clusters.summary: Voter turnout in the Kerala Assembly polls reached 78.03%, the highest in decades, with the elections held in a single phase. Across Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry, women voters outnumbered men in turnout, according to the Election Commission of India (ECI). Assam recorded 85.38% turnout, while Puducherry saw 89.83%, both logging record figures.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has proposed a one-hour delay for high-value digital transactions above ₹10,000 as part of measures to curb digital payment fraud, according to multiple reports. The RBI has invited public feedback on the proposal, with the deadline set for May 8. The suggestions also include additional checks, particularly for senior citizens, amid rising digital fraud incidents.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) conducted a ₹2-lakh crore Variable Rate Reverse Repo (VRRR) auction to absorb surplus liquidity, marking its first such move this year. This action led to a rise in bond yields and swap rates, reflecting tighter liquidity conditions in the market. Multiple reports confirm that the RBI's liquidity withdrawal contributed to increased overnight rates.

story_clusters.summary: The Supreme Court rejected a plea by a Tamil Nadu woman whose name was deleted from the electoral rolls, despite her claim that authorities had promised protection against "mass exclusion." articles.title: Insolvency And Bankruptcy Code (Amendment) Act, 2026 – Comprehensive Analysis
articles.summary: Executive Summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (Amendment) Act, 2026 (enacted April 2026) implements a series of extensive reforms to India's corporate insolvency regime. Building on the IBC 2016 framework and prior amendments, the new Act introduces creditor-driven mechanisms (notably a Creditor-Initiated Insolvency Resolution Process, CIIRP), strengthens creditor and Committee of Creditors (CoC) powers, tightens timelines, and broadens scope (e.g. for group and cross-border insolvency).
articles.raw_summary: Executive Summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (Amendment) Act, 2026 (enacted April 2026) implements a series of extensive reforms to India's corporate insolvency regime. Building on the IBC 2016 framework and prior amendments, the new Act introduces creditor-driven mechanisms (notably a Creditor-Initiated Insolvency Resolution Process, CIIRP), strengthens creditor and Committee of Creditors (CoC) powers, tightens timelines, and broadens scope (e.g. for group and cross-border insolvency).

articles.title: RBI cancels the licence of National Urban Cooperative Bank Ltd., Pratapgarh
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI), vide order dated April 10, 2026, has cancelled the licence of  National Urban Cooperative Bank Ltd., Pratapgarh , under Section 22 read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (BR Act). Consequently, the bank ceases to carry on banking business, with effect from the close of business on April 10, 2026. The Commissioner and Registrar of Cooperative, Uttar Pradesh has also been requested to issue an order for winding up the bank and appoint a liquidator

articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant stated that courts should grant anti-arbitration injunctions only in exceptional circumstances to preserve the autonomy of arbitral tribunals. The CJI also emphasized that arbitral tribunals must retain control over the use of technology and AI tools in their proceedings. This ruling underscores the balance between judicial oversight and the independence of arbitration processes in India.

This judgment impacts how tax officers handle cash deposit cases linked to demonetization. The ruling was delivered in response to a tax dispute involving cash deposits d

articles.title: At ICA conference, CJI Surya Kant underscores arbitration’s role in global economy
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant stated at an arbitration conference that the term 'Alternative Dispute Resolution' may need to be reconsidered, as arbitration is no longer an alternative but a primary method of resolving disputes. The CJI emphasized arbitration's growing importance in the global economy and its role in reducing litigation burdens. The remarks were made during a discussion on the evolving landscape of dispute resolution in India.
articles.raw_summary: Chief Justice Kant expressed that, “it may be time to rethink the very expression ‘Alternative Dispute Resolution’ since it is no longer an alternative to litigation”.

articles.title: Why is Iran not reopening the Strait of Hormuz? Unseen dangers at sea
articles.summary: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed due to naval mines, posing risks to global oil trade. Iran cannot fully reopen it due to technical and security challenges, while the U.S. rejects Iran's proposed transit fees, insisting the waterway is international. The situation highlights ongoing geopolitical tensions that could disrupt India's energy imports and maritime trade.
articles.raw_summary: The Strait of Hormuz remains a critical flashpoint, with Iran's inability to fully reopen it due to naval mines posing a significant threat to global trade. Technical limitations and ongoing security risks complicate mine-clearing efforts. US President Trump has rejected Iran's proposed transit fees, insisting the waterway is international.

articles.title: Section 80P Deduction Allowed as Bank Interest was Attributable to Business of Credit Society
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that bank interest earned by a credit co-operative society is classified as business income, not income from other sources. This allows the society to claim a full deduction under Section 80P(2)(a)(i) of the Income Tax Act. The decision clarifies tax treatment for co-operative societies and impacts their financial operations.
articles.raw_summary: The case examined classification of bank interest earned by a credit co-operative society. The Tribunal ruled it is business income and not income from other sources. The decision allows full deduction under Section 80P(2)(a)(i). The post Section 80P Deduction Allowed as Bank Interest was Attributable to Business of Credit Society appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: P&H HC GST Quashed Order Due to Failure to Consider Filed Reply on Portal in ITC Fraud Case
articles.summary: The Punjab and Haryana High Court quashed a GST order in an Input Tax Credit (ITC) fraud case because the adjudicating authority failed to consider the taxpayer's reply filed on the portal. The court emphasized that orders must account for all submissions before being passed. This ruling highlights the importance of procedural fairness in tax adjudication and could influence future GST enforcement practices.

articles.title: Penalty Upheld for Invoice Without Supply Due to Evidence of Paper Transactions
articles.summary: A tribunal upheld a penalty against a taxpayer for issuing invoices without actual supply of goods, citing lack of evidence like infrastructure and transport records. The ruling confirms non-genuine transactions, justifying the penalty under tax laws. The case highlights enforcement challenges in India's indirect tax regime, particularly around invoice fraud.
articles.raw_summary: The case examined whether goods were actually supplied against invoices. The Tribunal ruled that lack of infrastructure and transport evidence indicated non-genuine transactions, justifying penalty. articles.title: Tax Dues Not Secured Debt Under IBC – VAT First Charge Claim Rejected as IBC Prevails Over VAT Law
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that state VAT dues do not qualify as secured debt under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). The court rejected a claim for a first charge on VAT, affirming that the IBC takes precedence over state VAT laws. Tax dues were classified as operational debt in the resolution plan. This decision impacts how tax recoveries are treated during insolvency proceedings in India.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal ruled that state VAT provisions do not prevail over the Insolvency Code. Tax dues were rightly treated as operational debt under the resolution plan. The post Tax Dues Not Secured Debt Under IBC   VAT First Charge Claim Rejected as IBC Prevails Over VAT Law appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: General Reference To Tender Document Containing Arbitration Clause Will Not Amount To Its Incorporation In Contract : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has reiterated that a general reference in a Letter of Intent to an arbitration clause contained in a tender document cannot form a valid arbitration clause to seek an appointment of an arbitrator. A Bench of Justice J.K. Maheshwari and Justice Atul S. Chandurkar set aside a Bombay High Court order that had appointed an arbitrator in a construction dispute. The High Court had originally allowed the Respondent's plea for arbitration by concluding that a valid agreement existed.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has reiterated that a general reference in a Letter of Intent to an arbitration clause contained in a tender document cannot form a valid arbitration clause to seek an appointment of an arbitrator. A Bench of Justice J.K. Maheshwari and Justice Atul S. Chandurkar set aside a Bombay High Court order that had appointed an arbitrator in a construction dispute. The High Court had originally allowed the Respondent's plea for arbitration by concluding that a valid agreement existed.

articles.title: SC Upholds Non-Taxability of Online Platform Income Due to Failure of ‘Make Available’ Test
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld that income from online learning platforms is not taxable as 'Fees for Technical Services' (FTS) because the platform merely facilitates access to content without 'making available' technical services. The ruling clarifies that facilitation alone does not trigger FTS taxation under tax treaties. The decision impacts how digital education services are taxed in India.
articles.raw_summary: The case examined whether online learning services constitute technical services. The SC upheld findings that the platform only facilitated access to content. The ruling confirms that facilitation alone does not trigger FTS taxation. The post SC Upholds Non-Taxability of Online Platform Income Due to Failure of ‘Make Available’ Test appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Duty Liability Upheld as Notification Subsequent to Bill of Entry Filing Cannot Apply Retrospectively
articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that exemption benefits under a customs duty notification cannot be claimed if the notification is issued after the filing of Bills of Entry. The tribunal clarified that the duty liability is determined based on the date of filing the Bill of Entry, not the date of clearance. This decision impacts importers seeking retrospective duty exemptions and reinforces the principle that post-filing notifications cannot apply retroactively.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that exemption benefits cannot be claimed under a notification issued after the filing of Bills of Entry. It ruled that the relevant date for duty determination is the date of filing, not clearance. The post Duty Liability Upheld as Notification Subsequent to Bill of Entry Filing Cannot Apply Retrospectively appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: CESTAT Allowed Refund as Service Tax Credit Became Unavailable After GST Transition
articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal (CESTAT) ruled that refunds can be claimed for taxes paid under the pre-GST service tax regime if credits became unavailable after the transition to GST. The tribunal interpreted Section 142(3) of the CGST Act to allow such refund claims despite the repeal of earlier tax laws. The case was remanded back for further proceedings. This decision impacts businesses seeking tax refunds due to the GST transition.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal interpreted Section 142(3) of the CGST Act to allow refund of taxes paid under existing law. It held that such claims survive the repeal of earlier statutes. The decision clarifies tax credit eligibility for businesses undergoing expansion. The ruling was reported by TaxGuru.

articles.title: Service Tax Demand Set Aside as Confirmed Under Taxable Category not proposed in SCN
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that tax authorities cannot change the taxable category during adjudication if it was not originally proposed in the show cause notice (SCN). The tribunal set aside a service tax demand because it exceeded the scope of the notice. The ruling underscores that the SCN is the foundation for tax demands and cannot be altered later. This decision impacts how tax authorities frame and pursue tax demands in India.

A division bench of Justice Bharati Dangre and Justice Manjusha Deshpande refused to accept the contention of the Bank that it had sent SMS Alerts to petitioner customer - Subodh Korde, who was defrauded.

articles.title: Centre's fresh CAFE 3 brew seeks to ease life for car cos
articles.summary: The Centre has introduced new CAFE 3 norms, effective from April 2027, to reduce fuel consumption and CO2 emissions in the automotive sector. Carmakers will earn credits for producing efficient vehicles, EVs, and hybrids, with additional 'super credits' for zero-emission technologies. A three-year compliance block and credit trading system will provide flexibility. By 2031-32, CNG vehicles are expected to dominate the mix under these norms.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed an appeal because the petitioner's delay of 253 days exceeded the statutory condonable limit under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). The ruling underscores the strict enforcement of limitation timelines in legal proceedings. The decision reinforces the importance of adhering to procedural deadlines in insolvency cases.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court refused to entertain the appeal as the delay exceeded the statutory condonable limit under IBC. The ruling reinforces strict adherence to limitation timelines. The post SC Dismisses Appeal Due to 253-Day Delay Under IBC Limitation Rules appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Undisclosed Income Additions Quashed as Revenue Failed to Corroborate Diary Entries
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal quashed undisclosed income additions after the Revenue Department failed to prove that diary entries found during a search belonged to the assessee. The tribunal ruled that ownership and connection must be established through evidence, not just assumed. This decision highlights the limitations of statutory presumptions under Section 292C of the Income Tax Act. The ruling impacts how tax authorities can attribute documents to taxpayers during investigations.
articles.raw_summary: The case examined whether documents found during search can be automatically attributed to the assessee. The Tribunal ruled that ownership and connection must be established through evidence. The decision underscores limits of statutory presumptions under Section 292C. The post Undisclosed Income Additions Quashed as Revenue Failed to Corroborate Diary Entries appeared first on TaxGuru .

The decision, based on Sections 129 and 130 of the IGST Act, limits the grounds for interception to matters examinable at that stage. This impacts tax enforcement and compliance for businesses moving goods across states in India.

The court emphasized that using company money for personal bail violates Section 185 of the Companies Act. This decision strengthens corporate governance and prevents financial misconduct by directors.

articles.title: Unexplained Cash Deposits Addition Reduced Because Only Peak Balance Taxable: ITAT Ahmedabad
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Ahmedabad upheld the validity of reassessment based on cash deposit information but reduced the taxable addition by applying the 'peak credit theory' due to withdrawal patterns. This ruling clarifies that only peak balances in cash deposits are taxable, not the entire unexplained deposits. The decision affects how tax authorities assess unexplained cash transactions and could influence future tax policy interpretations. The case was reported by TaxGuru
articles.raw_summary: ITAT confirmed validity of reassessment based on cash deposit information. articles.title: Sabarimala Day 3: Govt urges ‘desi lens’ for Articles 25-26, says SC orders relied on Western definition
articles.summary: A nine-judge Supreme Court bench led by CJI Surya Kant is hearing a reference on the interplay between Articles 25 and 26 of the Indian Constitution, which deal with religious freedom. The government has urged the court to adopt an 'Indian lens' for interpreting these articles, arguing that the Supreme Court's previous orders relied on Western definitions. The case has significant implications for religious practices and constitutional rights in India.
articles.raw_summary: The interplay between Articles 25 and 26 is central to the Sabarimala reference, which a nine-judge Supreme Court bench led by CJI Surya Kant is hearing.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a state cannot differentiate between employees and pensioners when granting Dearness Allowance (DA). The ruling impacts pensioners' rights and state government policies on DA distribution.

articles.title: Reopening Invalid as Based on Issue Already Decided in Earlier Year: Gujarat HC
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court ruled that reopening a tax case based on an issue already decided in an earlier year is invalid. This decision impacts tax administration and legal precedents in India.
articles.raw_summary: The case examined reopening based on a prior disallowance under Section 80IB(10). The Court found that the disallowance had already been reversed by appellate authorities. articles.title: So, Here's the Problem: Textile Factories in 10 Nations Face Inconsistent Environmental Permit Rules, UN Study Finds
articles.summary: A UNEP report found that environmental permitting systems in ten major textile-producing nations, including India, have significant gaps in regulating industrial pollution from textile factories. Most national laws do not classify textile activities as environmentally significant or consider gendered impacts on workers. The study highlights inconsistencies in how spinning mills, dye houses, and garment factories are regulated, raising concerns about worker safety and environmental compliance.
articles.raw_summary: Environmental permitting systems across ten major textile-producing nations contain significant gaps in how industrial pollution from spinning mills, dye houses and garment factories is regulated, a new UNEP report has found. Most national legislation fails to classify textile activities as environmentally significant or to consider gendered impacts on workers.

articles.title: Survivor's Testimony Unimpeached, Non-Examination Of Mother Not Fatal: Allahabad HC Upholds Conviction In 1984 Rape Case
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court on Wednesday upheld the conviction of a man in connection with a 1984 Rape case while relying upon the unimpeached testimony of the survivor. A bench of Justice Manoj Bajaj added that non-examination of the mother of the survivor and another witness who had also arrived at the place of occurrence was not fatal to the case of the prosecution in view of the fact that other evidence was consistent with the guilt of the accused. The bench thus dismissed the accused's appeal

articles.title: Unrealized MTM Gains on Forward Contract Not Taxable before maturity: Kerala HC
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court ruled that unrealized mark-to-market (MTM) gains on forward contracts are not taxable before maturity, as they do not constitute real income until realized. The decision addresses a key tax policy question affecting financial markets and corporate accounting practices in India. The ruling clarifies the tax treatment of such gains, which has implications for businesses and investors relying on forward contracts for hedging or speculation.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether mark-to-market gains on forward contracts are taxable before maturity. articles.title: Much Ado About Rs.80/-
articles.summary: The Punjab government introduced a fee of Rs. 80 for downloading FIRs from its Sanjh Portal, which faced immediate backlash from social activists, politicians, and advocates. The government clarified that the fee would not apply to complainants or the accused but justified the charge by stating that free access was financially burdensome. The policy was withdrawn shortly after implementation due to the strong opposition.
articles.raw_summary: The move by Punjab Government to impose Rs.80 for downloading a FIR from its Sanjh Portal met with such a resistance that it had to be withdrawn no sooner than it was implemented. It was quite surprising that a small amount of Rs.80/- could have invited such an outrage from the social activists, Politicians and Advocates. State government tried to reason out by telling that this charge would not apply to the complainant and the accused. It further reasoned that free access was actually burdening

This initiative aims to strengthen India's EV value chain and reduce dependency on imports.
articles.raw_summary: Mines Secretary Piyush Goyal said that the proposed policy is at an advanced stage, and will begin with focus on Nickel and Lithium, with both minerals being used within EVs and Nickel being particularly used in high-end EVs.

articles.title: GST Registration cannot be cancelled for NIL Return: Uttarakhand HC
articles.summary: The Uttarakhand High Court ruled that the Goods and Services Tax (GST) registration of a taxpayer cannot be cancelled solely for filing nil returns. The court declared that the cancellation order and show cause notice were legally unsustainable because nil returns do not fall under the grounds specified in Section 29(2) of the GST Act. This judgment impacts tax administration and compliance for businesses filing nil returns under GST.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that filing NIL returns does not fall within the grounds specified under Section 29(2) of the GST Act. As a result, the cancellation order and show cause notice were declared legally unsustainable. The case has drawn attention to gender rights and constitutional protections.

articles.title: In Assam, first evicted, now erased
articles.summary: In Assam, over 2,000 Bengali-speaking Muslims in Kachutali village, near Guwahati, lost their voting rights after their names were removed from electoral rolls during a revision. The removals, which followed evictions from government land, have left many fearing they could be rendered non-citizens. The incident raises concerns about civil liberties and electoral fairness in the state.
articles.raw_summary: After a revision of electoral rolls in Assam, many people, mostly Bengali-speaking Muslims evicted from government land, lost their voting rights and now hold voter IDs deemed invalid. The fear of being made non-citizens is strongest at Kachutali village, about 30 km east of Guwahati, where more than 2,000 voters could not be a part of the democratic process of electing a new government, reports Rahul Karmakar

articles.title: Urgent cases to be mentioned only before CJI-led bench: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court (SC) has ruled that urgent cases must now be mentioned only before a bench led by the Chief Justice of India (CJI), even if the CJI is unavailable or presiding over a constitution bench. This changes the usual practice where urgent matters were handled by the seniormost available judge. The decision impacts how cases are prioritized and listed for hearing at the apex court.

The move aims to ensure adequate fuel supply for fertilizer production, which is critical for India's agricultural sector.

articles.title: Non-Refund of Investor Money is Continuing Offence, Small Amount Not a Defence: Calcutta HC
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court ruled that the failure to refund investor money is a continuing offence, meaning the limitation period does not apply until the funds are repaid. The court rejected the argument that the small amount involved could serve as a defence. This decision underscores the persistent nature of non-compliance in financial matters until rectified.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether failure to refund investor funds is time-barred. The Court held it is a continuing offence, rejecting the limitation defence. The key takeaway is that non-compliance persists until repayment is made. The post Non-Refund of Investor Money is Continuing Offence, Small Amount Not a Defence: Calcutta HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Bombay HC Lays Down Test for Rejection of Election Petitions
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has established a legal test to determine whether election petitions can be rejected. The Court must assess if the petition, even if unopposed and all claims are accepted as true, would still justify a favorable verdict for the petitioner. If not, the petition lacks a valid cause of action. This ruling impacts how election disputes are handled in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Court must examine whether the petition, even if taken as unopposed and all averments are accepted as true, would entitle the petitioner to a favourable verdict; if not, it discloses no cause of action. The post Bombay HC Lays Down Test for Rejection of Election Petitions appeared first on SCC Times .

The government's stance highlights the tension between constitutional secularism and religious freedom.

articles.title: From diversity to monotony, ecological communities are homogenising
articles.summary: The article explores biotic homogenisation, where diverse ecosystems worldwide are losing unique species and becoming increasingly similar due to environmental changes. This process replaces sensitive endemic species with common generalist ones, raising concerns about ecological balance and biodiversity loss. The phenomenon is documented globally but has implications for India's environmental policies and conservation strategies.
articles.raw_summary: Imagine walking through different ecosystems across the world, only to find that species inhabiting them look strikingly similar. This is the reality of biotic homogenisation, a process where diverse biological communities become increasingly similar as unique endemic species, highly sensitive to environmental changes, are replaced by common generalist species, which are insensitive to such perturbations. [ ]

articles.title: Can't have different DA hikes for staff and pensioners: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the government cannot provide different dearness allowance (DA) hikes for serving employees and pensioners, calling the 14% DA for staff and 11% dearness relief (DR) for retirees arbitrary and discriminatory. The court stated that inflation affects both groups equally, violating the right to equality. While financial constraints may justify delays in implementation, unequal benefit rates are not permissible. The ruling underscores the need for equitable treatment in
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that differential dearness benefit increases for serving employees and pensioners are arbitrary. Inflation impacts both equally, making a 14% DA hike for serving staff and only 11% DR for retirees discriminatory. The court stated that while financial constraints can justify implementation delays, unequal benefit rates violate the right to equality.

articles.title: India’s forex reserves up by $9.06 billion to $697.12 billion in week ending April 3

This rise indicates a stabilization in the country's financial position.

articles.title: China moves out of factory deflation after 3 years as Iran war impacts producers
articles.summary: China has moved out of factory deflation for the first time in three years after the war in Iran drove up energy costs. Producer prices rose by 0.5% in March, though consumer inflation slowed and manufacturers' profits remained weak. The conflict in Iran has disrupted global energy supplies, which could have broader economic implications for countries like India that rely on oil imports.
articles.raw_summary: China exits industrial deflation as war in Iran drives energy costs up, producer prices rise 0.5% in March, but consumer inflation slows and manufacturers profits stay weak

AERA is the body responsible for setting airport tariff regulations in India.

An alternative candidate, Somnath Dutta, will now contest the seat for TMC.

articles.title: Government plans weekly system to track EXIM trends
articles.summary: The Indian government plans to set up a weekly system to track export-import trends and industry stress, led by the commerce ministry. This move aims to address challenges faced by exporters, such as rising packaging costs and shipping disruptions, which are affecting sectors like apparel, leather, and medical devices. The initiative is part of efforts to streamline cargo handling procedures amid global tensions and cost pressures threatening domestic businesses.
articles.raw_summary: The govt will establish a system to monitor export-import trends and industry stress weekly. This comes as global tensions and rising costs threaten domestic businesses. The commerce ministry is working to streamline procedures for cargo handling. Discussions focused on challenges faced by exporters, including packaging material costs and shipping disruptions. Industries like apparel, leather, and medical devices are particularly vulnerable.

articles.title: Reliance rations fuel at Jio-BP petrol pumps as shortages deepen
articles.summary: Reliance Industries' Jio-BP petrol pumps are rationing fuel by capping purchases at ₹1,000 per visit due to deepening shortages. Operators are enforcing this limit to curb panic buying, though Reliance has not issued a formal directive. The move highlights growing fuel supply constraints that could prompt government or regulatory responses.
articles.raw_summary: While Reliance Industries has issued no formal directive, operators of Jio-BP petrol pumps have begun enforcing a ₹1,000/visit cap to curb panic buying.

articles.raw_summary: The announcement of the ceasefire will not determine the trajectory ahead, but by what follows within its limited duration. Two weeks is not a resolution horizon. It is a decision window.

articles.title: 'Dowry Death' Offence Not Attracted On Mere Demand Of Valuables Sans Cruelty/Harassment Nexus: Allahabad High Court
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court has observed that to attract an offence under Section 304-B IPC (Dowry Death) there should be a 'perceptible nexus' between the death and dowry related harassment or cruelty inflicted upon the victim. articles.title: Recyclable Materials Mean Nothing if the Product Cannot Be Disassembled
articles.summary: Performance outerwear, due to its complex multi-material construction and bonded components, is nearly impossible to recycle. A new research project is testing whether redesigning products for easier disassembly could solve this challenge. The apparel industry's technical complexity has long made end-of-life recovery difficult, but this initiative aims to change that from the design stage. The outcome could inform future policies on sustainable fashion and textile waste management.
articles.raw_summary: Performance outerwear is among the most technically complex categories in apparel—and among the hardest to recycle. Multi-material construction, bonded membranes, and inseparable trims have long made end-of-life recovery a structural impossibility rather than a logistical inconvenience. A new research and development project is testing whether design for disassembly can change that calculus from the ground up.

articles.title: The Sourcing Story: Industry Rewrote Its Geography Without Rewriting Its Dependence
articles.summary: The global garment industry has relocated apparel assembly to new countries over the past decade due to trade tensions and compliance pressures, but key inputs like fabrics and fibres remain concentrated in China. New research shows how production networks have reconfigured without reducing dependence on Chinese industrial inputs. This shift could impact India's role in global supply chains, given its growing manufacturing sector and trade policies. The findings underscore the need for India to
articles.raw_summary: The global garment industry has spent a decade reconfiguring its production geography in response to trade tensions, compliance pressures, and the demands of fast-moving online retail. New research now provides a detailed account of what has changed and what has not. Apparel assembly has moved, measurably and deliberately. The industrial inputs that make that assembly possible—fabrics, fibres, accessories—have stayed in China.

articles.title: Global sea surface temperatures are reaching record highs [Commentary]
articles.summary: Global sea surface temperatures (SST) are reaching record highs, driven by climate change. Oceans absorb most of the planet's excess heat, but the consequences of rising SST—such as marine ecosystem disruptions and extreme weather—are often overshadowed by discussions on air temperatures. This trend underscores the urgent need for climate action, including policy measures to address ocean warming and its impacts.
articles.raw_summary: Rising Sea Surface Temperatures (SST) and their seasonal fluctuations have emerged as among the most visible indicators of a warming planet. Yet, while public discourse on global warming tends to focus on rising air temperatures driven by anthropogenic greenhouse gas emissions, the increase in SST and its profound consequences remain underemphasised. Oceans absorb the vast [ ]

articles.title: ADB raises India’s FY27 growth forecast to 6.9%
articles.summary: India's economic growth forecast for FY27 has been raised to 6.9 percent by the Asian Development Bank. This upward revision is driven by increasing consumption, higher investment, supportive government policies, and recent trade pacts. While FY26 is projected at 7.6 percent, FY27 anticipates a moderation due to global uncertainties. The outlook for FY28 is a robust 7.

articles.title: Himanta wife passport row: Telangana HC gives Pawan Khera 1-week protection, tells him to move Assam court

The crisis stems from geopolitical tensions affecting fuel supply chains, with potential implications for India's energy imports and aviation sector.

The development could impact India's energy costs and economic policies, given the country's heavy reliance on oil imports.

articles.title: West Asia conflict: Govt to setup weekly monitoring mechanism to track exim trends
articles.summary: The Indian government is setting up a weekly system to monitor export-import trends and industry stress, aiming to address supply chain disruptions and rising costs affecting sectors like apparel and medical devices. Customs procedures for hazardous cargo will also be streamlined. The initiative seeks to improve transparency and efficiency in shipping and logistics, ensuring smoother trade operations.

The initiative aims to boost transparency and efficiency in shipping and logistics, ensuring smoother trade operations.

articles.title: Calm before storm? Axis Bank's Neelkanth Mishra says India must brace for geopolitical shocks every 1–2 years
articles.summary: Axis Bank Chief Economist Neelkanth Mishra warned that India should brace for geopolitical shocks every 1–2 years, calling current tensions part of a broader US–China “grand war.” While markets stabilised after a ceasefire push, Mishra cautioned that disruptions to supply chains, energy and trade could intensify, demanding faster reforms and resilience-building.

articles.summary: Telangana will conduct the first phase of the digital Census 2027 from May 11 to June 9, followed by a second phase in February 2027. The state has deployed 88,000 staff and will open a self-enumeration portal from April 16 to May 10. This marks India's first digital census effort, aiming to modernize data collection nationwide.

articles.summary: Assam, Puducherry, and Kerala recorded high voter turnout in assembly elections, with Kerala seeing the highest turnout in decades. The high participation was partly attributed to special revisions of electoral rolls in Kerala, Puducherry, and Assam. These administrative measures ensure accurate voter lists, which are critical for fair elections. The elevated turnout reflects strong public engagement in the electoral process.
articles.raw_summary: A significant factor in the elevated percentages was the Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls in Kerala and Puducherry, and Special Revision in Assam

articles.title: The three market outcome scenarios modelled by JPMorgan on the US-Iran war
articles.summary: JPMorgan has modeled three potential market outcomes based on a US-Iran war scenario. A bullish outcome would involve de-escalation in West Asia or a US Federal Reserve policy shift, leading to a broad market rally, lower bond yields, falling oil prices, tighter credit spreads, and a weaker US dollar. The scenarios are speculative and depend on geopolitical developments and central bank actions.
articles.raw_summary: A bullish scenario will be when there is proper de-escalation in West Asia or there is a policy pivot by the US Federal Reserve. In such a scenario, they see a broad "everything rally" where equities advance, bond yields drop, oil prices fall, credit spreads tighten and the US Dollar weakens.

This move aims to curb food fraud and protect consumer health under India's food safety laws.

articles.summary: Aditya Birla Real Estate will redeem ₹250 crore worth of 8.05% non-convertible debentures early by exercising a call option, with May 4 set as the redemption date. Shares of Aditya Birla Real Estate Ltd ended lower on Thursday, April 9, by 2.07% at ₹1,289.40 on the NSE.

articles.title: India resumes Iranian oil imports after 7 years as US eases sanctions
articles.summary: India has resumed importing Iranian oil for the first time in seven years after the US temporarily eased sanctions, as refiners seek to offset supply disruptions caused by tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.
articles.raw_summary: India has resumed importing Iranian oil for the first time in seven years after the US temporarily eased sanctions, as refiners seek to offset supply disruptions caused by tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: India Inc ramps up exports as Iran ceasefire reopens Middle East trade
articles.summary: Indian businesses are seizing opportunities after the Iran war ceasefire. Exports are resuming, and factories are returning to full capacity. Companies expect a surge in orders for reconstruction activities in war-affected nations. Pharma and packaged food sectors see increased demand.

articles.title: India bonds hemmed in by heavy supply, fragile truce
articles.summary: Indian government bonds remained steady Friday, impacted by a large sale of the benchmark 10-year note and worries over high oil prices due to a fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Traders awaited the auction results, anticipating potential yield increases. Meanwhile, easing liquidity pushed overnight index swap rates lower, as a temporary truce offered some relief.

articles.title: Amid West Asia war, FY26 goods exports may fall 2-3%: FIEO
articles.summary: India's goods exports may see a 7-8% drop in March due to the West Asia conflict. Full fiscal year 2026 exports could be 2-3% lower. However, overall goods and services exports are projected to rise 5-6% for FY26. Rising raw material prices and shipping costs are impacting shipments. Exporters seek government intervention on interest rates and policy simplification.

articles.title: Wholesale rice prices up 7% as West Asia ceasefire fuels export rush
articles.summary: Indian rice prices have seen a significant jump at the wholesale level. This surge follows a recent ceasefire between the US and Iran, leading to a rush of export orders. The impact is expected to be felt by consumers soon. Disruptions had previously caused prices to fall.

articles.title: West Asia crisis: Piyush Goyal meets Kuwait, UAE to restore trade, energy flows
articles.summary: India's Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal met with leaders from Kuwait and the UAE. Discussions focused on restoring trade and energy supplies disrupted by recent events. India offered assistance to Kuwait for food security. Both sides emphasized dialogue and diplomacy. Efforts are underway to ensure smooth trade flows and continuity of essential supplies.

articles.title: Global growth to slow to 3.2% in 2026, amid largest energy shock on record due to West Asia crisis: S&P Global
articles.summary: Global economic growth is expected to slow to 3.2% in 2026 due to the largest energy shock on record caused by the West Asia crisis. The conflict is disrupting oil production and key infrastructure, raising energy costs for net importers like Europe and Asia. India, as a major energy consumer, could face higher fuel prices and supply chain disruptions. The situation may prompt policy responses to mitigate economic and energy security risks.
articles.raw_summary: Global economic growth faces a slowdown to 3.2 percent in 2026. The West Asia conflict is causing the largest energy shock on record. Shipping, energy supply, and trade are impacted. Oil production and key infrastructure face disruptions. Europe and Asia, net energy importers, will see higher costs. This situation significantly alters the global economic outlook.

articles.title: Israel-Iran war: India rapeseed sales shift to private traders as war lifts edible oil prices
articles.summary: Indian farmers are selling rapeseed to private traders. Market prices are higher than government rates. This is due to a rally in edible oil prices linked to the Iran war. Higher rapeseed oil supplies are limiting edible oil imports. This trend is easing reliance on costly palm oil, soy oil, and sunflower oil imports.

Higher rapeseed oil supplies are limiting edible oil imports. This trend is easing reliance on costly palm oil, soy oil, and sunflower oil imports.

articles.title: Exporters' body FIEO sees 2-3 pc fall in goods exports during 2025-26
articles.summary: Indian merchandise exports are projected to decline by 2-3% in 2025-26. Global economic uncertainties, amplified by the West Asia crisis, are the primary drivers. This situation has already impacted shipments, increasing freight and raw material costs. While goods exports face a downturn, services exports are anticipated to grow. Exporters hope for stabilization following a ceasefire announcement.

articles.title: India has ample buffers to weather headwinds from Middle East conflict: World Bank
articles.summary: The World Bank stated India is well-positioned to handle the global energy shock due to strong buffers like high foreign exchange reserves and fiscal space. Despite global headwinds and trade turbulence, India's economy entered the current Middle East crisis from a position of strength, supported by robust domestic demand and export resilience.

articles.title: Tiruppur garment orders drop 15% as US, Europe demand weakens amid Iran War
articles.summary: Tiruppur's garment exports fell by 15% in March due to reduced demand from the US and Europe, where buyers held existing stock amid inflation concerns. Demand from West Asia also halted because of the Iran war, disrupting trade routes and increasing costs. Manufacturers are facing rising input prices. The situation may prompt discussions on trade policies or support measures for exporters.

Singhania advises investors to stay connected to resilient businesses for future returns.

articles.title: World Bank: South Asian labour market pivoting towards skills that can be amplified by AI
articles.summary: The World Bank's Chief Economist Franziska Ohnsorge noted that hiring has slowed in occupations exposed to AI, with job prospects eroding due to long-standing labour market disparities. This trend is part of a broader shift in South Asian labour markets toward skills that can be enhanced by AI. The findings suggest potential challenges for workforce adaptation and policy interventions in regions like India.
articles.raw_summary: World Bank Group's Chief Economist Franziska Ohnsorge said that hiring has slowed down in most AI-exposed occupations or firms with large productivity gains in that industry, with job prospects in AI-exposed occupations being eroded with persistence of long-standing sub-national labour market differentials.

articles.title: Army delayed strike during Op Sindoor at terror camps to respect namaz, reveals General Dwivedi
